name: crisis-mental-health
description: Immediate mental health crisis resources, warmlines, 988 navigation, involuntary hold rights, and alternatives to police welfare checks. For coordinators encountering acute distress.
source: Liberation Labs mutual aid knowledge base

triples.subject: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline
triples.object: 24/7 crisis counseling by phone (dial 988), chat (988lifeline.org), or text (text 988)

triples.subject: Crisis Text Line
triples.object: texting HOME to 741741 — connects to trained crisis counselor via text
988 routes to mental health professionals, not police — designed to de-escalate without law enforcement

triples.subject: peer support warmline
triples.object: people in distress who are NOT in imminent danger — emotional support, not crisis intervention

triples.subject: warmline
triples.object: staffed by peers with lived experience, lower intensity, available when you need to talk but aren't in crisis

triples.subject: community crisis response team
triples.object: police welfare check — unarmed mental health professionals respond to non-violent crises

triples.subject: involuntary psychiatric hold (5150/302)
triples.object: imminent danger to self or others AND professional assessment — cannot be applied for sadness or unusual behavior alone

triples.subject: person on involuntary hold
triples.object: attorney, hearing within statutory timeframe (typically 72 hours), and challenge the hold before a judge

triples.subject: safety planning
triples.object: identifying warning signs, coping strategies, people to contact, professionals to call, and making the environment safe

triples.subject: means restriction
triples.object: temporarily removes access to lethal means during crisis — reduces impulsive acts without long-term restriction

triples.subject: trauma response
triples.object: dissociation, hypervigilance, emotional numbness, flashbacks, or panic — these are normal responses to abnormal events

triples.subject: panic attack
triples.object: a heart attack or medical emergency (though it feels like one) — peaks within 10 minutes, passes within 30

triples.subject: grounding technique
triples.object: dissociation or panic — 5-4-3-2-1 (name 5 things you see, 4 you hear, 3 you touch, 2 you smell, 1 you taste)

triples.subject: mandatory reporting
triples.object: a person expresses specific plan and intent to harm self or identified others — not for general distress or past ideation

triples.subject: substance use crisis
triples.object: SAMHSA helpline (1-800-662-4357), local detox programs, naloxone access for opioid overdose

triples.subject: naloxone (Narcan)
triples.object: over-the-counter at pharmacies — reverses opioid overdose, safe to administer even if opioids aren't involved

triples.subject: Veterans Crisis Line
triples.object: dialing 988 then pressing 1, or texting 838255 — specialized support for veterans and service members

triples.subject: Trans Lifeline
triples.object: peer support for trans people in crisis at 877-565-8860 — staffed by trans people, no non-consensual active rescue

triples.subject: Trevor Project
triples.object: crisis intervention for LGBTQ+ youth at 1-866-488-7386, text START to 678-678, or chat at TrevorProject.org

triples.subject: crisis stabilization unit
triples.object: emergency room — short-term (23-hour) facility for psychiatric stabilization without ER wait times and costs

triples.subject: mobile crisis team
triples.object: 988, local crisis hotline, or community mental health center — comes to the person's location

triples.subject: after a crisis
triples.object: connecting to ongoing care within 48 hours — the period after crisis resolution has elevated risk

triples.subject: coordinator
triples.object: promise confidentiality if someone is in imminent danger — be honest about what you can and cannot keep private

triples.subject: compassion fatigue
triples.object: crisis responders and coordinators — secondary trauma from repeated exposure to others' crises is real and requires self-care

triples.subject: psychiatric advance directive
triples.object: a person to document treatment preferences while stable, for use during future crises when they may not be able to communicate
